evals.name: resume-from-chat-content
evals.prompt:
i need a resume PDF asap, can u make one. here's my stuff:

name: Dima Volkov
role: senior software engineer, looking for staff roles
email: dima@oqva.io
phone: (+1) 415-555-0143
github: dimavolkov
linkedin: dimavolkov
location: SF Bay Area

jobs:
- staff eng at OQVA, jan 2024 - now. built the entire workers infra for the tablet monitoring system. cut alerting latency from 12 min to under 30 sec. mentored 3 engineers.
- senior at Binatrix, 2021 - 2023. led migration off vercel onto cloudflare workers + d1. shipped a clerk auth integration that handles 50k MAU.
- backend eng at Acme, 2019 - 2021. wrote the entire deployment pipeline from scratch. ~200 deploys/month with zero rollbacks.

education:
B.S. Computer Science, Stanford, 2015 - 2019

skills: typescript, rust, go, cloudflare workers (deep), aws, postgres, d1, clerk, n8n

put the PDF somewhere obvious. it's for a staff role at notable-company. classic look pls, nothing too colorful.
evals.expected_output: Compiled cv.pdf in cwd using @preview/modern-cv with Dima's info filled in, font preflight handled (likely NCM override), preview PNG rendered, absolute path reported.

assertions.text: A non-trivial .pdf file (>= 5 KB) was produced
TRANSCRIPT.md contains an absolute /Users/dima/... path to the PDF

evals.name: cover-letter-typst
evals.prompt: write me a cover letter PDF for the Senior Platform Engineer role at Cloudflare. background: I've been doing edge-first architecture for 5 years, shipped a Workers-based monitoring system that handles 12k devices, comfortable with R2/D1/Durable Objects. recipient is just 'Hiring Team, Cloudflare, San Francisco'. my name is Dima Volkov, dima@oqva.io. keep it under one page, professional tone.
evals.expected_output: Compiled cover-letter.pdf using modern-cv letter mode, single page, today's date, professional tone, preview rendered, absolute path reported.

assertions.text: A non-trivial .pdf file (>= 5 KB) was produced
The .typ mentions Workers or edge architecture
The .typ uses datetime.today() or a 2026 date

evals.name: generic-pdf-from-prose
evals.prompt: I have this short summary of our Q4 architecture decisions. Can you give me a PDF version I can hand to my boss? [markdown with ## ### headings, '<5%' and '<30 sec' inline]
evals.expected_output: Compiled output.pdf using a hand-rolled document template (NOT modern-cv/neat-cv). Markdown headings converted to Typst =/==/===. Angle brackets handled without label-parsing errors. Preview rendered, absolute path reported.

assertions.text: A non-trivial .pdf file (>= 5 KB) was produced
The .typ does NOT import modern-cv or neat-cv
The .typ has top-level (=) and sub-level (==) headings
Compile succeeded — angle brackets did not block compilation
